_comment: Synthetic patient store for UMA integration testing. Inspired by publicly available EHR patterns (MIMIC-III, OMOP CDM). All data is fictional. Patient: Arjun Mehta, 62M, diabetic with hypertension, recent hospitalization.

docs.title: Comprehensive Metabolic Panel + HbA1c
docs.summary: HbA1c mildly elevated at 7.4% (target <7.0% for T2DM). Fasting glucose 148 mg/dL. eGFR 62 mL/min/1.73m² — Stage 2 CKD. LDL 98 mg/dL on statin. Potassium 4.1 mEq/L. Creatinine 1.3 mg/dL.
docs.markdownArtifact:
# Comprehensive Metabolic Panel + HbA1c
**Date:** 15 April 2026 | **Lab:** Apollo Diagnostics, Mumbai

## Glycaemic Control
| Test | Result | Reference | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| HbA1c | **7.4%** | 4.0–5.6% | HIGH |
| Fasting Glucose | **148 mg/dL** | 70–99 mg/dL | HIGH |

HbA1c target for T2DM patients is generally <7.0%. Current reading of 7.4% suggests suboptimal glycaemic control — medication review advised.

## Lipid Panel
| Test | Result | Reference | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| LDL | 98 mg/dL | <100 mg/dL | NORMAL |
| HDL | 42 mg/dL | >40 mg/dL | NORMAL |
| Total Cholesterol | 182 mg/dL | <200 mg/dL | NORMAL |
| Triglycerides | **164 mg/dL** | <150 mg/dL | HIGH |

## Kidney Function
| Test | Result | Reference | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Creatinine | **1.3 mg/dL** | 0.7–1.2 mg/dL | HIGH |
| eGFR | **62 mL/min/1.73m²** | >60 | BORDERLINE |
| Microalbumin/Cr Ratio | **62 mg/g** | <30 mg/g | HIGH |

Microalbuminuria (62 mg/g) with eGFR 62 indicates early diabetic nephropathy. Nephrology referral and ACE-inhibitor/ARB coverage recommended.

## CBC
All values within normal limits.

## Thyroid
TSH 2.1 mIU/L — normal thyroid function.

docs.title: HbA1c + Lipid Panel (Oct 2025)
docs.summary: HbA1c 7.8% — above target. LDL 112 mg/dL — requires statin optimisation. Triglycerides 198 mg/dL.
docs.markdownArtifact:

Glycaemic control worse than target. Statin dose may need optimisation given LDL 112 and HDL 39.

docs.title: Prescription — Dr. Nair (April 2026)
docs.provider: Dr. Priya Nair, Diabetologist, Kokilaben Hospital
docs.summary: Review after latest labs. Metformin continued. Glimepiride dose increased to 2mg. Added Empagliflozin 10mg for HbA1c reduction and cardio/renal protection. Rosuvastatin 20mg continued. Telmisartan 40mg for BP and nephroprotection. Aspirin 75mg. Vitamin D3 60000IU weekly × 8 weeks.
docs.markdownArtifact:
# Prescription — Dr. Priya Nair
**Date:** 15 April 2026 | **Doctor:** Dr. Priya Nair, Diabetologist | **Hospital:** Kokilaben Dhirubhai Ambani Hospital, Mumbai

**Diagnosis:** Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (uncontrolled), Hypertension, Stage 2 CKD, Dyslipidaemia

## Medications
1. **Metformin 500mg** — BD (twice daily with meals) — continue
2. **Glimepiride 2mg** — OD before breakfast — INCREASED from 1mg
3. **Empagliflozin 10mg** — OD — NEW (SGLT2 inhibitor; renal + cardiac protection)
4. **Rosuvastatin 20mg** — OD at bedtime — continue
5. **Telmisartan 40mg** — OD morning — continue
6. **Aspirin 75mg** — OD after breakfast — continue
7. **Vitamin D3 60,000 IU** — once weekly × 8 weeks

## Instructions
- Aim for HbA1c <7.0% by October 2026
- Monitor fasting glucose daily; target 80–130 mg/dL
- Low-salt, low-GI diet; 30 min walk daily
- Recheck labs in 3 months
- Nephrology referral for microalbuminuria

**Next appointment:** 15 July 2026

docs.title: Echocardiogram Report
docs.summary: Mild LVH (LV mass index 118 g/m²). EF 58% — normal systolic function. Mild diastolic dysfunction (Grade 1). No valvular disease. Aortic root 34mm — normal. Conclusion: Hypertensive heart disease, mild LVH, preserved EF.
docs.markdownArtifact:
# Echocardiogram Report
**Date:** 20 March 2026 | **Facility:** Hinduja Hospital Cardiac Unit
**Referring Physician:** Dr. Priya Nair

## Findings
- **LV dimensions:** EDD 50mm, ESD 34mm (normal)
- **LV mass index:** 118 g/m² (normal <95 F, <115 M) — MILDLY ELEVATED
- **Ejection Fraction (EF):** 58% — NORMAL systolic function
- **Diastolic function:** Grade 1 diastolic dysfunction (impaired relaxation)
- **Valves:** No significant valvular disease
- **Aortic root:** 34mm — normal
- **Pericardium:** Normal

## Impression
Mild LVH consistent with hypertensive heart disease. Preserved systolic function. Mild diastolic dysfunction — continue BP management aggressively.

**Recommended follow-up:** Repeat echo in 12 months.

docs.title: Complete Blood Count + Iron Studies
docs.summary: Mild anaemia — Hb 11.8 g/dL. Low serum ferritin 14 ng/mL — iron deficiency anaemia. Transferrin saturation 16%. Folate and B12 normal. WBC normal.
docs.markdownArtifact:
# CBC + Iron Studies
**Date:** 10 February 2026 | **Lab:** SRL Diagnostics

| Test | Result | Reference | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hemoglobin | **11.8 g/dL** | 13.5–17.5 | LOW |
| MCV | **74 fL** | 80–100 | LOW |
| Ferritin | **14 ng/mL** | 30–400 | LOW |
| Serum Iron | **52 μg/dL** | 60–170 | LOW |
| Transferrin Sat | **16%** | 20–50% | LOW |
| Vitamin B12 | 412 pg/mL | 200–900 | NORMAL |
| Folate | 9.8 ng/mL | 3.1–17.5 | NORMAL |

**Impression:** Microcytic hypochromic anaemia consistent with iron deficiency. Oral iron supplementation recommended. Rule out GI blood loss given CKD + aspirin use.

docs.title: Discharge Summary — Hinduja Hospital
docs.summary: 5-day admission for hypertensive urgency (BP 188/112) with chest tightness. Managed with IV labetalol, oral amlodipine added. Echo showed mild LVH. Discharged stable with BP 138/88. CABG history noted from 2018.
docs.markdownArtifact:
# Discharge Summary
**Admission:** 23 Nov 2025 | **Discharge:** 28 Nov 2025 | **Hospital:** Hinduja Hospital, Mumbai
**Consultant:** Dr. Rajesh Kumar, Cardiologist

## Presenting Complaint
BP 188/112 mmHg with chest tightness and headache. No STEMI on ECG.

## Past Medical History
- Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (since 2018)
- Hypertension (since 2016)
- CABG × 2 vessels (2018, Lilavati Hospital)

## Hospital Course
Admitted for hypertensive urgency. Managed with IV labetalol, BP controlled within 24h. Echo showed mild LVH with preserved EF. Troponin negative × 3. Discharged with BP 138/88 on amlodipine added to existing regimen.

## Discharge BP: 138/88 mmHg

## Discharge Medications
1. Telmisartan 40mg OD
2. **Amlodipine 5mg OD** (new)
3. Metformin 500mg BD
4. Aspirin 75mg OD
5. Rosuvastatin 20mg OD

docs.title: Prescription — Dr. Rajesh Kumar (Nov 2025, post-discharge)
docs.provider: Dr. Rajesh Kumar, Cardiologist, Hinduja Hospital
docs.summary: Post-discharge cardiac review. BP 142/90. Amlodipine dose increased to 10mg. Continue existing medications. Advised daily BP monitoring at home.
docs.markdownArtifact:
# Post-discharge Review — Dr. Rajesh Kumar
**Date:** 12 December 2025

## Vitals
- BP: 142/90 mmHg (improved but target <130/80)
- HR: 76 bpm, regular

## Changes
- Amlodipine increased from 5mg to 10mg for better BP control
- Continue all other medications unchanged

## Advice
- Home BP monitoring twice daily (morning + evening)
- Low-sodium diet (<2g/day)
- Regular 30-min walk
- Return if BP >160 or symptomatic

**Next review:** 3 months
